2024-01-23.abstract: A U.N. office said Israel’s detention and treatment of detainees might amount to torture. It estimated thousands had been detained and held in “horrific” conditions. Some were freed wearing only diapers.

2024-01-24.title: Israeli Soldiers Clearing Buffer Zone in Gaza Die in Blast
2024-01-24.abstract: On the deadliest day for Israelis since the ground invasion against Hamas began, about 20 soldiers were killed as they prepared to level buildings near the border.

2024-01-24.title: Court Finds Trudeau Overreached by Using Emergency Law to End Blockade
2024-01-24.abstract: The government said it will appeal the decision that came two years after the start of a trucker protest that paralyzed the downtown of Canada’s capital.

2024-01-24.title: 6-Day Rail Strike Halts Most Trains in Germany
2024-01-24.abstract: The walkout, over pay and hours, is the most significant in years at the national train operator, which warned of a “massive reduction” in service.

2024-01-24.title: The U.S. strikes 2 more Houthi targets in Yemen.
2024-01-24.abstract: The attacks — the ninth in two weeks — hit anti-ship missiles that the Iran-backed militia could use against merchant and Navy ships in the region.

2024-01-24.title: Man Arrested on 77 Counts of Murder in Johannesburg Building Fire
2024-01-24.abstract: The police said a 29-year-old had confessed to starting the August fire that tore through a derelict, overcrowded building occupied by poor people.

2024-01-25.title: U.N. Shelter in Besieged Khan Younis Is Struck, and at Least 9 Die
2024-01-25.abstract: United Nations officials said a building that was being used as a shelter by about 800 people had been “clearly marked” as one of their facilities. Israel denied responsibility.

2024-01-25.title: Israel Tries to Rebut Genocide Charge by Declassifying Cabinet Decisions
2024-01-25.abstract: South Africa has accused Israel of committing genocide in Gaza. Israel’s legal team has challenged the charge by providing the court with secret orders made by Israel’s civilian and military leaders.

2024-01-25.title: ‘No Time to Go Wobbly’: Why Britain Is Lobbying U.S. Republicans on Ukraine
2024-01-25.abstract: The U.K., often a wingman to the United States in defense, is pushing its ally to stand firm against President Vladimir V. Putin, amid fears that Russia poses an existential threat to Europe.

2024-01-25.title: Top Official’s Resignation Reveals Tension Atop European Soccer
2024-01-25.abstract: The official, Zvonimir Boban, said he was quitting UEFA because of a proposal that could allow the organization’s leaders to extend their terms, and their power.

2024-01-25.title: Will a New Monument for Those Enslaved by France Heal or Divide?
2024-01-25.abstract: The memorial will list the names of those freed in 1848, a design some say does more to glorify France for abolition than to atone for slavery itself.

2024-01-26.title: Indigenous Australians Plan to Go Bigger on Australia Day
2024-01-26.abstract: “Invasion Day is the reason why we’re all here today, but we must go beyond that,” one activist said.

2024-01-26.title: In Angola, a Former Cold War Rival, Blinken Touts U.S. Investments
2024-01-26.abstract: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken finished a four-nation tour of Africa with a visit to Angola, site of major new U.S. investments, which may help counter China’s influence.

2024-01-26.title: Kenyan Court Blocks Deployment of Police Force to Haiti
2024-01-26.abstract: A contingent of 1,000 Kenyan officers was intended to lead a multinational force financed by the United States to restore security in a Caribbean country terrorized by armed gangs.

2024-01-26.title: King Charles Admitted to U.K. Hospital for Prostate Procedure
2024-01-26.abstract: Buckingham Palace said the king was being treated at a London hospital. Catherine, Princess of Wales, remains hospitalized after abdominal surgery last week.

2024-01-26.title: A Child of Another War Who Makes Music for Ukrainians
2024-01-26.abstract: Mirza Ramic, a Bosnian who sought refuge in the United States, is bringing his electronic music to Ukraine, “to show my support in these hard times.”

2024-01-27.title: U.N. Court Orders Israel to Prevent Genocide, but Does Not Demand Stop to War
2024-01-27.abstract: The top United Nations court in The Hague did not rule on whether Israel was committing genocide in Gaza, the accusation that South Africa brought before the court.

2024-01-27.title: China Acknowledges Imprisoning a British Man on Spy Charges
2024-01-27.abstract: A businessman who had worked in China for decades vanished from view in 2018, but his fate had been unknown, and publicly unremarked upon, until now.

2024-01-27.title: London’s Highline Will Echo Its New York Inspiration, With Local Notes
2024-01-27.abstract: Hoping to repeat the success of Manhattan’s park, London is transforming a disused rail line, elevated 25 feet above the city’s streets, into its own floating green space.

2024-01-27.title: When Soccer’s Content Mine Loses Sight of Reality
2024-01-27.abstract: As documentaries and series about teams proliferate, it’s easy to pine for the ones that offered the clearest windows into the game.

2024-01-27.title: FIFA Convictions Are Imperiled by Questions of U.S. Overreach
2024-01-27.abstract: Two Supreme Court decisions and a lower court’s ruling have cast doubt on the legal basis for a host of prosecutions. Several defendants want their records cleared and their money back.

2024-01-28.title: ‘Two Days of Horror’: Doctor Documents Scene at Nasser Hospital
2024-01-28.abstract: Dr. Ahmed Moghrabi, a surgeon who has worked at Nasser Medical Complex for three years, said that the “pressure is very big” at the hospital as Israeli forces surrounded Khan Younis, Gaza.

2024-01-28.title: Finland’s Presidential Election, First Since Joining NATO, Heads to Runoff
2024-01-28.abstract: Voting took place as the alliance’s newest member grapples with concerns about potential aggression from its neighbor Russia.

2024-01-28.title: For New Moms in Seoul, 3 Weeks of Pampering and Sleep at a Joriwon
2024-01-28.abstract: Some new mothers say postpartum care centers are the best part of childbirth in South Korea, where fewer people are deciding to have children because of high costs.

2024-01-28.title: What It Took Young People in China to Get Their Jobs
2024-01-28.abstract: For many job seekers, the toughest market in years is exacting a physical and emotional toll.

2024-01-28.title: Where Is Hamas Getting Its Weapons? Increasingly, From Israel.
2024-01-28.abstract: The very weapons that Israeli forces have used to enforce a blockade of Gaza are now being used against them.

2024-01-29.title: Details Emerge on U.N. Workers Accused of Aiding Hamas Raid
2024-01-29.abstract: Israeli officials have presented evidence they say ties workers at a Palestinian aid agency in Gaza to violence during the Hamas-led attack on Israel.

2024-01-29.title: For Europe and NATO, a Russian Invasion Is No Longer Unthinkable
2024-01-29.abstract: Amid crumbling U.S. support for Ukraine and Donald Trump’s rising candidacy, European nations and NATO are making plans to take on Russia by themselves.

2024-01-29.title: Princess of Wales Returns Home From Hospital After Abdominal Surgery
2024-01-29.abstract: The sudden hospitalization of Catherine, the wife of Prince William, in mid-January, surprised royal watchers in Britain. She will convalesce for two to three months.

2024-01-29.title: Iran Denies Ordering Drone Strike as Biden Weighs a Response
2024-01-29.abstract: The deaths of three U.S. service members were the first known fatalities from hostile fire since the start of Israel’s war against Hamas.

2024-01-29.title: Russian Figure Skater Is Handed Four-Year Ban in Olympic Doping Case
2024-01-29.abstract: Kamila Valieva, once a 15-year-old gold medal favorite, was punished in a case that upended the Beijing Games, and kept other athletes from receiving medals.

2024-01-30.title: Colombia, a Usually Wet Nation, Reels Amid Widespread Wildfires
2024-01-30.abstract: Firefighters, many of them volunteers, have been confronting dozens of blazes amid high temperatures this month. The conditions have been linked to climate change.

2024-01-30.title: Canada Delays Plan to Offer Medically Assisted Death to the Mentally Ill
2024-01-30.abstract: A parliamentary panel concluded that there are not enough doctors, particularly psychiatrists, in the country to properly assess patients.

2024-01-30.title: Northern Ireland’s D.U.P. Signals End to Political Deadlock After 2 Years
2024-01-30.abstract: The Democratic Unionist Party said it had agreed a deal to return to power-sharing after negotiating with the British government.

2024-01-30.title: If It Isn’t Perfect, Is It Still K-Pop?
2024-01-30.abstract: Musical acts like Balming Tiger are challenging the idea that K-pop is nothing but polished, perfectly synchronized boy bands and girl groups.

2024-01-30.title: Aid Group Calls Halts in Funding for UNRWA ‘Reckless’
2024-01-30.abstract: The U.S. and some other donor countries said they would suspend funding for the agency after Israel charged that a dozen of its workers had played a role in the Oct. 7 attack or its aftermath.

2024-01-31.title: Why Tourists Have Been Stranded Near Machu Picchu
2024-01-31.abstract: Demonstrators protesting a new ticketing system blocked access to Peru’s most-visited site and threw rocks at police officers, who fired tear gas into the crowd.

2024-01-31.title: The Farmers’ Protests Have Become a Wildfire. He Was the Spark.
2024-01-31.abstract: Jérôme Bayle had enough of the hardships of French farming, so he blocked a highway with friends. Hundreds of other farmers have followed suit.

2024-01-31.title: In His Second Sentencing in Two Days, Imran Khan Gets 14 Years
2024-01-31.abstract: The sentences, just over a week before Pakistan’s national election, come as the former prime minister’s party has been battered by a crackdown.

2024-01-31.title: Thai Court Rules Progressive Party’s Reform Push Violated Constitution
2024-01-31.abstract: Critics call the verdict against the popular Move Forward Party, which hoped to scale back a royal defamation law, another blatant attempt to block the people’s will.

2024-01-31.title: An Israeli Charity for Palestinians Grapples With Oct. 7 Attacks
2024-01-31.abstract: Before the war, Road to Recovery helped bring sick Gazans to Israeli hospitals. Several of its volunteers died in the Hamas attacks, and donations to the group have slowed.
